questions.question: What was the landmark in the image original main function?
questions.expected: The Brandenburg Gate originally served as a ceremonial city gate and entrance to Berlin.
questions.rgb_answer: The Brandenburg Gate was originally a triumphal arch, serving as the gateway to the royal palaces and gardens in Berlin.

gt_answers.Small: The Brandenburg Gate was originally a triumphal arch, a monumental gateway that once stood in the center of Berlin. It was built in 1791 to commemorate the Prussian victory over Napoleon and was one of the most famous landmarks in Berlin. The gate was also used as a symbol of peace and unity, and it played a significant role in German history, including being the site where the Berlin Wall was torn down in 1989.
gt_answers.Medium: The Brandenburg Gate was originally a triumphal arch, intended to celebrate the victory of King Frederick William I of Prussia over the French in 1760. It was designed by architect Georg Wenzeslaus von Knobelsdorff and sculpted by Johann Gottfried Schadow. The gate was part of the city wall of Berlin and was meant to commemorate the Prussian victory and the military prowess of the king. Over time, it became a symbol of Berlin and Germany, and during the Cold War, it was a significant landmark on the "Wall" that divided East and West Berlin.
gt_answers.Large: The Brandenburg Gate was originally a triumphal arch, intended to celebrate the victory of King Frederick William I of Prussia over the French in 1760. It was designed by architect Georg Wenzeslaus von Knobelsdorff and sculpted by Johann Gottfried Schadow. The gate was part of the city wall of Berlin and was meant to commemorate the Prussian victory and the military prowess of the king. Over time, it became a symbol of Berlin and Germany, and during the Cold War, it was a significant landmark on the "Iron Curtain" that divided East and West Berlin.
